data.name: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS)
data.description: Using magnetic fields to stimulate or suppress neural activity in specific brain regions
data.status: FDA approved for depression (2008), OCD (2018), smoking cessation (2020), anxious depression (2021)
data.breakthrough: Stanford Accelerated Intelligent Neuromodulation Therapy (SAINT) achieved 79% remission in treatment-resistant depression in 5 days

data.name: Transcranial Ultrasound Stimulation (TUS)
data.description: Focused ultrasound to modulate deep brain structures without surgery, with millimeter precision
data.status: Clinical trials for depression, essential tremor, pain; research expanding rapidly
data.breakthrough: 2023 studies showed TUS can modulate thalamus and basal ganglia non-invasively with lasting effects

data.name: Closed-Loop Deep Brain Stimulation (DBS)
data.description: Adaptive DBS that monitors brain activity in real-time and adjusts stimulation parameters accordingly
data.status: Medtronic Percept PC FDA approved 2020; adaptive DBS clinical trials ongoing
data.breakthrough: Adaptive DBS reduces Parkinson's symptoms 50% more efficiently than continuous stimulation while using less battery

data.name: Neuroprosthetic Memory Implant
data.description: Hippocampal prosthesis that encodes and decodes memory signals to restore memory formation
data.developer: USC / Wake Forest (Dong Song, Sam Deadwyler)
data.status: Primate and human research; demonstrated memory improvement in epilepsy patients
data.breakthrough: 2023: Hippocampal prosthesis improved short-term memory by 15-20% and long-term memory by 25-30% in human patients

data.name: Optogenetics Therapy
data.description: Using light-sensitive proteins delivered by gene therapy to precisely control specific neuron types
data.status: First human optogenetics therapy (retinitis pigmentosa) showed partial vision restoration in 2021
data.breakthrough: 2021: Blind patient partially recovered vision using optogenetic therapy combined with light-amplifying goggles

data.name: Neural Dust / Neural Lace
data.description: Sub-millimeter wireless neural sensors ('motes') distributed throughout brain tissue, powered and read by ultrasound
data.developer: UC Berkeley (Michale Maharbiz), Iota Biosciences (now part of Synchron)
data.status: Animal demonstrations; human applications years away
data.breakthrough: Demonstrated wireless recording from peripheral nerves in rats using sub-mm motes

data.name: Digital Twin Brain Models
data.description: High-resolution computational models of individual brains for surgical planning and treatment optimization
data.developer: Human Brain Project, Virtual Epileptic Patient (Jena), Allen Institute
data.status: Clinical validation for epilepsy surgery planning; expanding to other conditions
data.breakthrough: Virtual Epileptic Patient model predicts seizure onset zones with 85%+ accuracy, guiding surgery
